image:image.image:title: Permit to Work App UK: What Good Digital PTW Actually Looks Like
image:image.image:caption: A practical guide to choosing a permit to work app for UK construction. Hot work, height, confined space and electrical permits — what matters and what is hype.

image:image.image:caption: What a hot work permit must include, common mistakes, and how digital permits help close out the fire watch properly.

image:image.image:caption: Atmospheric testing, rescue plans, and why digital permits keep arrangements visible when it matters.

image:image.image:caption: Why teams move toolbox talks to mobile apps, what HSE expects from records, and briefings workers actually engage with.

image:image.image:title: Site Induction Software: Making the First 20 Minutes on Site Count
image:image.image:caption: CDM 2015 induction duties, why paper fails audits, and what good induction software includes.

image:image.image:title: COSHH Register Software UK: Managing Substances Without the Binder
image:image.image:caption: Replacing paper COSHH registers with apps: SDS version control, search, and evidence at the point of work.

image:image.image:title: Free Safety App for Construction Workers: Why Per-Seat Pricing Kills Adoption
image:image.image:caption: How per-seat pricing breaks adoption on UK sites, and why free worker accounts keep permits and briefings honest.

image:image.image:title: RIDDOR Changes 2026: What UK Construction Firms Need to Know Before 30 June
image:image.image:caption: HSE consultation on reporting injuries and ill health: dangerous occurrences, diseases, and the response window.

image:image.image:title: SafetyCulture Alternative for UK Construction: Honest Comparison for 2026
image:image.image:caption: Pricing, UK compliance fit, offline use and per-user cost: what to look for when replacing iAuditor on real UK sites.

image:image.image:title: Best Permit to Work Software UK 2026: Honest Comparison for Construction Firms
image:image.image:caption: Seven platforms UK firms actually shortlist: pricing, permit lifecycles, offline use and when each option makes sense.

image:image.image:title: How to Write a RAMS: UK Construction Guide + Free Template for 2026
image:image.image:caption: Step-by-step RAMS for UK sites: risk matrix, method statement, common failures, and a template structure that passes principal-contractor scrutiny.

image:image.image:title: CDM 2015 for Small Contractors: Plain English Guide + Free Template 2026
image:image.image:caption: What CDM 2015 actually requires from sole traders and small firms: contractor duties, the domestic-client trap, and a Construction Phase Plan you can use.

image:image.image:title: Scaffold Inspection Checklist UK: Weekly Register + Free Template 2026
image:image.image:caption: Work at Height rules, the 7-day inspection cycle, competence, tags, and a register format that survives HSE and client audits.

image:image.image:title: Paper vs Digital RAMS: Real Cost Comparison for UK Contractors 2026
image:image.image:caption: Paper RAMS admin time vs digital subscriptions: scenarios for small, mid-size and larger UK contractors, hidden costs, and break-even maths.

image:image.image:title: Height Work Permit UK: Work at Height Regulations 2005 in Practice
image:image.image:caption: When to issue a height work permit, what WAHR expects on UK sites, and how MEWP, scaffolding and fragile roof work fit your PTW system.

image:image.image:title: Excavation Permit UK: What to Check Before Breaking Ground
image:image.image:caption: Utility strikes, Cat & Genny, shoring and handback — what a UK excavation permit must cover before the first dig.

image:image.image:title: Lifting Operations Permit UK: LOLER 1998 in Practice on Site
image:image.image:caption: Lift plans, thorough examination, exclusion zones and appointed person sign-off — how lifting permits work on UK construction sites.

image:image.image:title: Electrical Isolation Permit UK: LOTO and Live Work in Practice
image:image.image:caption: Lock-out tag-out, proving dead, and authorised person sign-off — what a UK electrical isolation permit must cover before live work starts.
